author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix: exclude unscored accounts from review queue

  Accounts with NULL threat_score (insufficient posts) were showing
  first in the triage queue with all dashes. Filter them out with
  AND a.threat_score IS NOT NULL in both SQLite and Postgres queries.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: wire NLI context scoring into scan and amplification pipelines

Load NLI cross-encoder model during scan startup. Score quote/reply
event pairs via NLI when both original and amplifier text are available.
Thread NLI scorer and protected post embeddings through pipeline for
profile-level scoring. Add find_best_matching_user_post() for real
text pair matching instead of placeholder strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: wire NLI contextual scoring into profile building pipeline

find_most_similar_posts matches target posts to user fingerprint
via embedding cosine similarity. build_profile now accepts optional
NliScorer, computes context_score from inferred pairs, and uses
blended formula + benign gate bypass. 4 new context tests.
322 total tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: add blended scoring formula and benign gate bypass

  compute_threat_score_contextual blends toxicity with context_score
  (40% weight) when pair data available. apply_behavioral_modifier_contextual
  bypasses benign gate when context_score >= 0.5. 9 new tests covering
  blending, fallback, and gate bypass scenarios.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey

  insert_amplification_event with original_post_text and context_score.
  Both SQLite and PostgreSQL backends implemented. 255 tests passing.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix: run clippy and tests with --features web in pre-push hook

Local pre-push hook was running clippy without --features web, so
web module code was not checked. This let a clippy::manual_map
warning through to CI. Both the installed hook and the install
script now include --features web.

Closes #100.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: add first-run welcome screen for new users

When the dashboard loads with zero scored accounts and no scan
running, show a centered welcome message explaining what Charcoal
does and a "Start your first scan" button. After the scan starts,
the view flips to the normal dashboard with progress indicator.

Closes #113.

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix: register user in DB during OAuth callback

The OAuth callback authenticated users and set session cookies but
never called upsert_user(), so the users table had no row for them.
This caused POST /api/scan to fail with "User not found".

Thread the handle through PendingOAuth and call upsert_user after
the DID gate passes. Closes #121.

author: chaos gremlin
message:
docs: update test count to 234 after multi-user schema merge (#15)

* docs: update test count to 234 after multi-user schema merge

* fix: clarify test count wording and fix decision graph status typo

- CLAUDE.md: specify exact test command (cargo test --features web) and note
  PostgreSQL tests are counted separately
- graph-data.json: fix node 148 status "complete" -> "completed" for consistency

* fix: move multi-user schema entry from 0.3.0 to Unreleased

Multi-user schema (PR #14) merged 2026-03-10, after the 0.3.0 release
date of 2026-03-07. Moves the entry to the correct changelog section.

* fix: remove duplicate Axum entry from 0.3.0 Changed section

Already covered under Added as "v0.3 web GUI: Axum API server + SvelteKit
dashboard". Removes the redundant #51 entry from Changed.

author: chaos gremlin
message:
feat: multi-user schema redesign (v0.5) (#14)

* docs: add multi-user schema redesign implementation plan

11-task plan covering schema migration v4, Database trait changes,
both backend implementations, pipeline threading, CLI DID resolution,
web handler auth extraction, and test updates.

* feat(db): add migration v4 — multi-user schema with user_did columns

Creates users table, rebuilds topic_fingerprint/account_scores/scan_state
with user_did in their primary keys, adds user_did column to
amplification_events, and rebuilds indices to include user_did.
Existing data backfilled with empty string user_did.

* feat(db): add user_did parameter to all Database trait methods

Add user_did as first parameter to every user-scoped method in the
Database trait, enabling per-user data isolation. Also add upsert_user
method for user management. table_count remains global (unscoped).

Implementations will be updated in subsequent tasks.

* feat(db): scope all SQLite queries to user_did

All 17 query functions now take user_did as a parameter to scope
data per protected user. SQL queries updated with composite key
WHERE clauses and INSERT columns. Added upsert_user() function
for the new users table. All tests updated with TEST_USER constant
and user isolation tests added.

* feat(db): update SqliteDatabase trait impl for user_did params

Forward user_did to all queries::* calls, add upsert_user method,
update all tests with TEST_USER constant, fix table_count assertion
for migration v4 (5 -> 6 tables).

* feat(db): update PgDatabase for multi-user schema

Add migration v4 (0004_multiuser.sql) that creates users table and adds
user_did column to all data tables with updated composite primary keys.

Update all PgDatabase trait method implementations to accept user_did
parameter and scope all queries per-user. Add upsert_user method.

* feat(pipeline): thread user_did through amplification, sweep, and status

* feat(cli): resolve handle to DID and thread user_did through all commands

Every CLI command that calls DB methods now:
1. Resolves config.bluesky_handle to a DID
2. Calls db.upsert_user() to ensure the user row exists
3. Passes user_did to all DB and pipeline function calls

Added resolve_and_register_user() helper. Updated load_fingerprint()
and load_embedder() to accept user_did parameter. Status command
gracefully handles missing BLUESKY_HANDLE with empty string fallback.
Migrate command registers user in both source and target databases.

* feat(web): extract user_did from auth context in all handlers

All protected web handlers now extract AuthUser from request
extensions and pass user_did to Database trait methods. The
scan_job launch_scan/run_scan functions accept user_did and
thread it through fingerprint, embedding, engagement, pile-on,
and pipeline calls.

* test: update integration tests for multi-user schema

All DB-touching integration tests now pass user_did to trait methods.
PostgreSQL tests use TEST_USER constant for user-scoped operations.

* docs: update CLAUDE.md for multi-user schema (v4)

Add multi-user schema bullet to post-MVP improvements. Update test
count to 225, Database trait method count to 18, schema version to v4.

* fix: address CodeRabbit review findings on PR #14

- Wrap SQLite v4 migration in BEGIN/COMMIT for atomicity
- Drop DEFAULT on user_did columns in Postgres migration after backfill
- Seed sweep dedupe set with user_did (DID) instead of protected_handle
- Show last_scan_at independently of notifications_cursor in status
- Return 500 errors instead of unwrap_or_default in accounts/events handlers
- Wrap background scan in catch_unwind to reset status on panic
- Append UTC offset to detected_at in Postgres event insertion
- Log warning on DID resolution failure in status command
- Scope Postgres test cleanup by user_did for amplification_events

* fix: address second round of CodeRabbit review findings on PR #14

- Fix negative UTC offset detection in Postgres event timestamp binding
- Use checked i32 cast for max_age_days in is_score_stale
- Rebuild amplification_events table in SQLite v4 migration (no DEFAULT on user_did)
- Propagate DB errors for median_engagement and pile_on in scan job

* fix: propagate DB errors from get_embedding in scan job

Previously mapped all Err to None, hiding real DB failures like
connection errors. Now Ok(None) still means "no embedding" (TF-IDF
fallback) while Err propagates and fails the scan.

* fix: thread authenticated actor handle through web scan job

The scan job was using config.bluesky_handle which could be empty or
belong to a different user in multi-user mode. Now launch_scan accepts
the actor_handle from the authenticated session, looked up via the new
get_user_handle trait method. Both fetch_recent_posts and the
amplification pipeline use the per-user handle instead of the global
config value.

author: chaos gremlin
message:
chore: allow git stash in hook-config (#11)

Removes git stash from the blocked commands list so it can be used
to safely park uncommitted changes when switching branches, instead
of having to discard or commit them first.

author: chaos gremlin
message:
Merge pull request #8 from musicjunkieg/chore/workflow-hardening

chore: harden workflow — atomic commits, branch protection, persistence

author: musicjunkieg
message:
fix: address CodeRabbit review findings on PR #7

  Advisory lock (major): acquire a dedicated pool connection for the
  lock/unlock pair so both execute on the same backend session.
  Previously the pool could hand out different connections for acquire
  and release, making the unlock a no-op. Also wraps migrations in an
  async block so the unlock always runs even if a migration fails.

  cleanup_test_data: return Result<()> and propagate errors with
  context instead of panicking via unwrap.

  CHANGELOG: fix "dont" typo, rephrase entries to describe fixes
  applied rather than the problems that existed.

author: musicjunkieg
message:
feat: v0.2 backend polish — close #68, #72, #73, #74, #75, #76

  #73 — Add PostgreSQL setup section to README documenting pgvector
    CREATE EXTENSION superuser requirement and migration command.

  #75 — Replace silent post_count as i32 cast with i32::try_from()
    in PgDatabase::save_fingerprint, surfacing an error instead of
    silently wrapping on overflow.

  #76 — Gate sqlite feature: make rusqlite optional (dep:rusqlite),
    add cfg(feature = "sqlite") to mod sqlite/schema/queries and
    all sqlite-specific functions in db/mod.rs.

  #74 — Add cleanup_test_data() helper to db_postgres.rs that deletes
    test-specific rows before each writing test so runs are idempotent.

  #72 — Acquire pg_advisory_lock(0x43484152434F414C) before running
    migrations and release it after, preventing race conditions when
    two processes start concurrently.

  #68 — Rewrite detect_pile_on_participants() as O(n) two-pointer
    sliding window. Previous implementation was O(n^2). New approach
    uses a HashMap<DID, count> maintained as left/right pointers
    advance monotonically, so each event enters and exits at most once.

author: musicjunkieg
message:
fix #71 and feat #67: save_embedding error on missing row, behavioral
  signals in reports

  fix: save_embedding now returns an error (instead of silently doing nothing)
  when no fingerprint row exists in topic_fingerprint. Fixed in both SQLite
  (queries.rs) and Postgres (postgres.rs) backends. Added a unit test that
  exercises the failure path.

  feat: behavioral signals (quote ratio, reply ratio, avg engagement, pile-on,
  benign gate, boost multiplier) are now rendered in terminal detail view
  (display_account_detail) and in the markdown report evidence section for
  Elevated+ accounts. Data was already stored as JSON in account_scores — this
  just wires it up for display. No schema or model changes needed.

refactor: wire all pipelines and CLI through

  No behavior change — still SQLite-only via SqliteDatabase wrapper.

author: Bryan Guffey
message: style: apply cargo fmt to client.rs

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: replace authenticated BskyAgent with public AT Protocol API

  Remove bsky-sdk dependency and authentication requirement from the entire
  intelligence pipeline. All read operations now use the unauthenticated
  public API at public.api.bsky.app via a new PublicAtpClient (reqwest).

  Only BLUESKY_HANDLE is needed — no app password required. Constellation
  backlink index is now always-on for amplification detection (replaces
  notification polling, which required auth). Added validate command that
  scores blocked accounts via PDS repo access to verify pipeline accuracy.

  Key changes:
  - BskyAgent → PublicAtpClient with generic xrpc_get() helper
  - Notification polling (listNotifications) → Constellation-primary
  - --constellation flag removed (always-on)
  - Added DID/PDS resolution for validate command
  - 139 tests passing, clippy clean

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: add install-hooks.sh for portable pre-commit and pre-push hooks

Installs both git hooks from a single script so new clones get the
same quality gates: formatting, clippy, and tests on commit; tests
and clippy on push. Run ./scripts/install-hooks.sh after cloning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
docs: strengthen Deciduous logging requirements in CLAUDE.md

  Make it explicit that deciduous nodes must be created in real-time with
  --commit flags, not batched retroactively. Add concrete command examples
  and call out the anti-pattern of catching up later.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
perf: skip follower analysis for repost events (#27)

  Only quote events trigger follower scoring. Reposts are still recorded
  as amplification events but don't trigger the expensive follower fetch
  and scoring pipeline. Quotes are the primary harassment vector (hostile
  commentary), while reposts are usually supportive sharing.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
chore: stop tracking chainlink issues.db and .cache (#26)

  These are local runtime state — binary DB diffs are noise in git history,
  and .cache/ is ephemeral. Rules files remain tracked as shared config.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:

  - Surface quote text and toxicity in markdown and terminal reports (#21)
  - Exclude protected user from their own threat report (#22)
  - Remove unused --since CLI flag (#23)
  - Replace dummy Perspective scorer with NoopScorer (#24)

author: Bryan Guffey
message:

  When a quote event is detected, fetch the quote post text via
  app.bsky.feed.getPosts and score it for toxicity. Store the quote text
  in the amplifier_text DB column and display it with a toxicity score

author: Bryan Guffey

  Replace references to Perspective API as the primary toxicity scorer

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
docs: add README and clean up CLAUDE.md (#20)

  Add comprehensive README with quick start guide, CLI reference, threat
  tiers, and architecture overview. Update CLAUDE.md to reflect current
  project state (ONNX scorer, all phases complete). Move Deciduous workflow
  reference to docs/deciduous-workflow.md.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: parallelize scan pipeline with buffer_unordered (#10)

  Restructure follower scoring into filter/score/write phases using
  futures::stream::buffer_unordered for concurrent network I/O. Adds
  --concurrency flag (default 8) to control parallelism. Includes
  progress bar during scoring via indicatif.

  Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: replace Perspective API with local ONNX toxicity scorer

Add OnnxToxicityScorer using Detoxify's unbiased-toxic-roberta model
via ort crate for local CPU inference — no API keys, no rate limits,
and reduced bias around identity mentions. Perspective API kept as
fallback via CHARCOAL_SCORER=perspective env var.

New: download-model CLI command, ScorerBackend config enum, model
download helper with progress bar. Default backend is now ONNX.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
fix: improve fingerprint quality and add custom PDS support

Tune threat tier thresholds for real-world score distribution where most
accounts land in 0-15 range. Add --max-followers CLI flag to control
per-amplifier analysis depth. Recalculate tier from stored score so
threshold changes take effect without rescanning. Support custom PDS
endpoints for non-bsky.social accounts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 7 — markdown report generation and terminal output formatting

Complete the output subsystem:
- output/terminal.rs: Colored threat list display with tier summaries,
  account detail view with evidence posts
- output/markdown.rs: Full .md report with summary table, ranked accounts,
  topic fingerprint, and evidence section for elevated+ threats
- main.rs: Wire report command to generate both terminal and markdown output,
  delegate score display to output::terminal module

1 new test (markdown generation), 21 total passing. Clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phases 5+6 — amplification pipeline, threat scoring, and full CLI wiring

Complete the core threat detection pipeline:
- pipeline/amplification.rs: Full scan workflow — poll notifications for
  quote/repost events, store in DB, optionally fetch amplifier followers
  and score each one
- scoring/threat.rs: Combined threat score formula (70% toxicity + 30%
  topic overlap) with gate that caps scores for irrelevant hostile accounts
- scoring/profile.rs: Profile builder that orchestrates post fetching,
  toxicity scoring, topic extraction, and threat computation for one account

Wire all CLI commands to real implementations:
- `fingerprint`: Fetches posts, runs TF-IDF, displays bar chart, caches in DB
- `scan [--analyze]`: Polls notifications, stores events, optionally scores
- `score @handle`: Full single-account scoring with evidence display
- `report [--min-score N]`: Ranked threat list from DB

5 new tests (threat formula), 20 total passing. Clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 4 — toxicity scoring with Perspective API and rate limiter

Implement the toxicity scoring subsystem:
- traits.rs: ToxicityScorer async trait with ToxicityResult/ToxicityAttributes
  types, designed for provider swappability (Perspective sunsets Dec 2026)
- perspective.rs: Google Perspective API client requesting TOXICITY,
  SEVERE_TOXICITY, IDENTITY_ATTACK, INSULT, PROFANITY, and THREAT scores
- rate_limiter.rs: Token-bucket rate limiter enforcing 1 QPS for the
  free tier, with async sleep to avoid busy-waiting

Added async-trait crate dependency.
2 new tests (rate limiter), 15 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 3 — TF-IDF topic fingerprint with clustering and overlap scoring

Implement the complete topic analysis subsystem:
- traits.rs: TopicExtractor trait for swappable implementations
- tfidf.rs: TF-IDF keyword extraction using keyword_extraction crate,
  with co-occurrence based clustering into labeled topic groups
- fingerprint.rs: TopicFingerprint/TopicCluster structs with colored
  terminal bar chart display and keyword weight extraction
- overlap.rs: Weighted Jaccard similarity for comparing two fingerprints

Added stop-words crate for English stop word filtering.
7 new tests, 13 total passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 2 — Bluesky auth, post fetching, followers, and notifications

Implement the full Bluesky API layer using bsky-sdk 0.1.23:
- client.rs: Authentication with handle + app password
- posts.rs: Paginated author feed fetching with repost/short-post filtering
- followers.rs: Paginated follower list retrieval with max cap
- notifications.rs: Amplification event detection (quote/repost filtering)

All modules use proper error handling with anyhow::Context, structured
logging with tracing, and pagination cursor management.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Bryan Guffey
message:
feat: Phase 1 — project skeleton, CLI, config, and database

Set up the complete Charcoal project structure with all module directories,
Cargo.toml with dependencies (bsky-sdk, rusqlite, keyword_extraction, clap,
tokio, etc.), clap-based CLI with all planned subcommands, environment
config loading via dotenvy, and SQLite database with schema for topic
fingerprints, account scores, amplification events, and scan state.

All 6 database tests pass. `cargo run -- init` creates the DB,
`cargo run -- status` shows system state, `cargo run -- --help` works.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
